asamāhito.meaning: not concentrated; not tranquil, not composed
asamāhito.gram: past participle, nominative singular masculine, of na samādahati (na + saṁ + ā + √dhā)
breakdown: Concentrated, composed, collected; past participle of samādahati.

asantassa.meaning: of one who is not good; of non-peaceful character
asantassa.gram: present participle / adjective, genitive singular of asanta (na + santa)
breakdown: Genitive singular of santa “good, true, peaceful” (present participle of atthi).

asapattā.meaning: without enemies; not hostile; free from rivals

asataṁ.meaning: of those who are not good; the wicked; not being
asataṁ.gram: masculine, genitive/dative plural (or accusative singular) of asanta (na + santa)
breakdown: Oblique form of santa “good, true, existing” (present participle of atthi).

asatiamanasikāro āpajjitabbo.meaning: one should undergo the effort to not remember and not give attention
breakdown: Should be undertaken, should be entered upon; potential participle of āpajjati.

asaṅgati.meaning: non-meeting; absence of intercourse or union

asaṅkheyye.meaning: incalculable; literally “not to be calculated”; an immensely long period of time
asaṅkheyye.gram: neuter noun / potential participle of na saṅkhāti (na + saṁ + √khā), locative singular
breakdown: To be calculated, calculable; potential participle of saṅkhāti “to reckon”.

asecanakattāya.meaning: deliciousness; the quality of needing no addition
asecanakattāya.gram: neuter abstract noun, dative singular (na + secanaka + -tta)
breakdown: Needing sprinkling or admixture; from √sic “to sprinkle”.
Abstract suffix -tta in the dative singular: “for the state of”.

asesavacanametaṁ, nissesavacanametaṁ, ni.meaning: this is a saying without remainder, a complete saying, an unchangeable saying
breakdown: Unchangeable, not to be turned; direct, without figurative qualification.

asesavirāganirodho.meaning: the entire fading away and cessation; complete dispassion and extinction
asesavirāganirodho.summary: This compound describes the cessation of craving in the Third Noble Truth: its complete fading away and extinction without remainder.
breakdown: Absence of desire, dispassion, fading away.
asesavirāganirodho.goenka: This phrase appears in the Dhammacakkappavattana Sutta's statement of the Noble Truth of the Cessation of Suffering: it is the complete fading away and cessation of this very craving (taṇhā), forsaking it and giving it up, the liberation from it, leaving no place for it. The cessation is asesa — without any remainder — pointing to the total eradication of craving, not its mere suppression.

breakdown: Association with, frequenting; from sevati “to associate, serve”.
Belonging to Avanti, the western region.
And, also (the final vowels coalesce in sandhi).

assavanatā.note: divergence: the lexicon glosses “with attention, in attention”; DPD analyses the form as na + savana + tā, “the state of not listening”. The lexicon gloss is followed here.

assaṁ.meaning: I would be; if I were to be
assaṁ.gram: verb, optative 1st person singular of atthi (√as), irregular

asso bhadro kasāmivā.meaning: like a good horse touched by the whip; as a fine horse avoids the whip
asso bhadro kasāmivā.summary: A simile from the Dhammapada: as a good horse responds before the whip falls, an alert person gives no occasion for blame.
breakdown: As (iva) by the whip (kasā); “like [a horse touched] by the whip”.
Stem of assādeti “to taste, relish, enjoy” (ā + √sād).

assādeti.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular (ā + √sād + e + ti)

assāma.gram: verb, optative 1st person plural of atthi (√as), irregular

asādhāraṇo.meaning: special, peculiar, unique; not shared in common
breakdown: Common, shared, general; from sa + ā + √dhar.
By violence, by force; instrumental singular of sāhasa, from √sah.

asātattāya.gram: neuter abstract noun, dative singular (na + sāta + -tta)
breakdown: Abstract suffix -tta in the dative singular: “to the state of”.

atakkāvacaro.meaning: beyond the range of thought; unthinkable; not in the sphere of reasoning
atakkāvacaro.gram: adjective, nominative singular masculine, compound (na + takka + avacara)
breakdown: Sphere, realm, range (takka + avacara coalesces to takkāvacara).

athenena.meaning: not by theft; not as a thief
breakdown: Instrumental singular of thena “thief”; from √then “to steal”.

athāyaṁ.meaning: and then this; then this very

aticaritā.meaning: one who transgresses; one who commits adultery
aticaritā.gram: masculine agent noun (aticaritar); the lexicon also treats it as a participle of aticarati
breakdown: Goer, one who conducts himself; agent noun from carati “to move, behave”.
I am (1st person singular of atthi).
Quotation marker, fused by sandhi (asmi + ti > asmīti).

aticiraṁ.gram: adverb, accusative singular of aticira (ati + cira)

breakdown: Goes; from √gam “to go”; hence “goes beyond, overcomes”.
Prefix: beyond, over (with sandhi doubling of the k).

atikkameyya.gram: verb, optative 3rd person singular of atikkamati
breakdown: Prefix: beyond, over (with sandhi doubling of the k).
Surpassed, gone beyond; past participle of atikkamati.

atimaññeti.meaning: to despise; to be arrogant towards
atimaññeti.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, causative of atimaññati (ati + √man)
breakdown: Thinks (causative form); from √man “to think”; “to think oneself above”, hence to despise.
Harsh, rough, cruel; instrumental singular of pharusa.

atipāto.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular (from atipāteti; ati + √pat)
breakdown: Causing to fall; from √pat “to fall”; hence striking down, slaying (as in pāṇātipāta, killing living beings).
